Ocular Prosthetic Clinic in Kerala

The Ocular Prosthetic Clinic at Giridhar Eye Institute in Kerala is committed to restoring the appearance and function of the eye for individuals who have lost an eye due to trauma, disease, or congenital conditions. Our clinic specializes in custom-made ocular prosthetics, offering personalized solutions that enhance both aesthetics and confidence. We understand that losing an eye can be a traumatic experience, and our compassionate team is dedicated to providing the highest level of care, ensuring that each patient feels supported throughout the process. With our advanced technology and expertise, we create prosthetic eyes that are not only realistic in appearance but also comfortable and tailored to fit each patient’s unique anatomy.

Custom-Made Prosthetics

Each prosthetic eye is custom-made for natural symmetry and appearance.

Advanced Fitting Techniques

We utilize advanced technology for a comfortable, irritation-free fit.

Comprehensive Aftercare

We offer ongoing care and adjustments for optimal prosthetic eye performance.

Restored Confidence, Appearance

Our prosthetics enhance appearance and boost self-esteem in social interactions.

Your Trusted Partner in Ocular Prosthetics

For the best outcomes in ocular prosthetics, selecting the right clinic is essential. At Giridhar Eye Institute, we are committed to offering a patient-centered approach that emphasizes comfort, aesthetics, and long-term care. Our Kerala-based clinic is well-regarded for its expertise in creating lifelike prosthetic eyes that enhance the quality of life for our patients. Whether you need a prosthetic eye for the first time or are looking to replace an existing one, our team is here to assist you every step of the way. Trust in our experience and dedication to help you regain confidence and enjoy a renewed sense of normalcy.

Frequently Asked Questions

Find answers to common questions about ocular prosthetics, including fitting, care, and maintenance, to help you navigate your journey.

What is an ocular prosthesis?

An ocular prosthesis is an artificial eye designed to restore the appearance of a missing or damaged eye.

How is an ocular prosthesis fitted?

Fitting involves measuring and creating a custom prosthesis tailored to your eye socket for optimal comfort and appearance.

Is the fitting process painful?

The fitting process is generally painless. Local anesthesia may be used for comfort during the procedure.

How often do I need to replace my ocular prosthesis?

Ocular prostheses should be replaced every 5-7 years, or sooner if significant changes in the eye socket occur.

Can I wear my ocular prosthesis during activities?

Yes, most patients can wear their prosthesis during daily activities, including sports and swimming, with proper care.

 

How do I clean my ocular prosthesis?

Cleaning involves gently rinsing with water and using mild soap. Regular professional cleaning is also recommended.

 

Will an ocular prosthesis look natural?

Yes, ocular prostheses are custom-made to match the appearance of your natural eye, ensuring a realistic look.

 

Can I have an ocular prosthesis if I have a remaining eye?

Yes, patients with a remaining eye can have an ocular prosthesis for cosmetic reasons, ensuring balance and symmetry.
